title: I Dont Wanna Know Why The Caged Bird Sin

jokes.text: I am not an Fdic-insured bank.
jokes.setup: Chalkboard gag opening - Bart writing lines as punishment
jokes.punchline: Absurd financial disclaimer instead of typical school punishment
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons chalkboard gag with contemporary financial crisis reference. Economic and unexpected.

jokes.text: Think. Think. / I'm not thinking. You think. / You have to think, it's your job!
jokes.setup: Homer struggling to remember what tomorrow is
jokes.punchline: Homer refusing to think and making it Marge's responsibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - lazy, selfish, but in a recognizable domestic way.

jokes.text: To be fair, the millennium just started, so it's not that big a deal.
jokes.setup: Lisa receiving Student of the Millennium Award
jokes.punchline: Bart deflating the achievement with timeline logic
jokes.explanation: Sharp sibling deflation with actual logic. Good Bart characterization as the deflator.

jokes.text: What?! Name 12.
jokes.setup: Marge saying Homer misses too many precious moments
jokes.punchline: Homer's oddly specific demand for proof
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - defensive but weirdly specific. Excellent economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Lisa, if you like, I'm certified to conduct a mock father daughter conversation.
jokes.setup: Homer missing the Father-Daughter Dance
jokes.punchline: Marge offering to roleplay as Homer
jokes.explanation: Touching and funny - shows Marge trying to compensate for Homer's failures in a specific way.

jokes.text: American sober or Irish sober? / Point zero eight sober. / Point one five. / Point zero nine. / Point one zero with a stomach full of bread. My final offer. / Deal.
jokes.setup: Marge demanding Homer come sober to Lisa's event
jokes.punchline: Negotiating blood alcohol levels like a business deal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that treats alcoholism like contract negotiation. Perfect character voices and structure.

jokes.text: Ooh, an awesome accident. Easy, easy... I'll just take three seconds to gawk. Aw, everyone's okay.
jokes.setup: Homer rushing to be on time for Lisa's event
jokes.punchline: Stopping to rubberneck at accident, disappointed no one's hurt
jokes.explanation: Dark Homer characterization - his morbid curiosity overriding his good intentions.

jokes.text: 'Make way for Superman,' said Superman as he punched everyone at the Coffee Bean. Chapter Two...
jokes.setup: Homer having free time to work on his novel
jokes.punchline: Terrible Superman story with random violence
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's terrible creative writing - Superman as sociopathic coffee shop attacker.

jokes.text: I did both your grandmas. / Shut up!
jokes.setup: Background characters discussing something mundane
jokes.punchline: Sudden inappropriate sexual brag
jokes.explanation: Cheap shock humor that doesn't serve character or story. Random inappropriate comment.

jokes.text: Darn these long bank lines. Although it does give me a moment with my thoughts. Ooh... Darn these long bank lines.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about bank lines but finding silver lining
jokes.punchline: His thoughts are just the same complaint
jokes.explanation: Perfect circular Homer logic - his inner monologue is identical to his outer complaints.

jokes.text: Where's Dr. Kevorkian when you need him? / Well, if I know doctors, he's probably golfing.
jokes.setup: Old man boring Homer with building history
jokes.punchline: Death wish followed by casual doctor stereotyping
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about assisted suicide deflated by mundane doctor stereotype.

jokes.text: Uh, not a separate robbery we're together. / You mean like a couple? / No! / Yes! / Oh, great. We're being robbed by Johnny and Clyde!
jokes.setup: Bank robbers clarifying they're working together
jokes.punchline: Misunderstood as romantic couple, Homer's Bonnie and Clyde reference
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection comedy with cultural reference. Homer getting the reference wrong adds character.

jokes.text: We're being robbed by Johnny and Clyde!
jokes.setup: Bonnie and Clyde reference during bank robbery
jokes.punchline: Gender confusion creates 'Johnny and Clyde'
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay on famous criminal duo with gender swap

jokes.text: We didn't plan for this, man. We didn't plan for this. / Ol' Gil's here for his first day as security guard. Oh, boy...
jokes.setup: Robbers panicking about unexpected complications
jokes.punchline: Incompetent Gil arriving as the 'security'
jokes.explanation: Gil's perpetual bad timing and incompetence perfectly positioned for maximum irony.

jokes.text: Come on, Chief, you know this. / Oh, a shooty-stealy.
jokes.setup: Lou trying to get Wiggum to remember police code
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's childish slang term for armed robbery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chief Wiggum characterization - childish but oddly descriptive. 'Shooty-stealy' is instantly quotable.

jokes.text: Homer. / I'm a hostage in a bank robbery. / What?! / Oh, my God, my sweet Margie! Okay, listen very carefully. Don't do anything they say, remain panicky, and above all, try to be a hero.
jokes.setup: Marge calling Homer during bank robbery
jokes.punchline: Homer giving terrible hostage advice
jokes.explanation: Homer's advice is the opposite of what you should do as a hostage - perfect character stupidity.

jokes.text: If I don't make it, feel free to remarry from anyone on the list in the bulletin board in the closet. Lindsey Naegle, Booberella, Blythe Danner. Also, feed cat
jokes.setup: Marge giving final instructions in case she dies
jokes.punchline: Pre-prepared list of replacement wives including random actress
jokes.explanation: Absurd that Marge has a pre-planned remarriage list. Blythe Danner is a perfect random choice.

jokes.text: I was voted America's least funny clown. Worse than Scuzzo, Scummo, Oopsie, Carlos Mencia, Stinko, Blumpy. Even worse Sergeant Serious! How could I do worse than him?! I stole all his jokes!
jokes.setup: Krusty reassuring hostages he won't do anything funny
jokes.punchline: List of terrible clowns including real comedian, admission of joke theft
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with Carlos Mencia reference (topical joke stealing comedian). Krusty's self-awareness adds character depth.

jokes.text: Maybe you should take over, cause I got no stake in this. This isn't even my bank. I'm over at First Federal.
jokes.setup: Homer asking Wiggum to handle the hostage situation
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's disinterest due to banking with a competitor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum logic - treating police work like customer loyalty. Absurd but character-consistent.

jokes.text: Finally, the help we need a DVD of The Negotiator.
jokes.setup: Police need help with hostage negotiation
jokes.punchline: Relying on Hollywood movie for guidance
jokes.explanation: Decent satire of police incompetence and over-reliance on media for guidance.

jokes.text: Which chapter should we skip to? 'Meet Danny Ron', 'Trouble Brewing,' 'Off the Case', 'My Baby's in There,' 'Enter Niebaum,' 'Take the Shot,' 'Sabian's Choice,' 'Check and Mate,' 'Friends At Last,' 'Closing Credits'?
jokes.setup: Police using DVD of The Negotiator for hostage situation help
jokes.punchline: Treating movie like instruction manual with chapter selection
jokes.explanation: Great concept of using Hollywood for police training, though list runs a bit long

jokes.text: I shouldn't die. I just came here to check the date!
jokes.setup: Homer panicking about potentially being killed
jokes.punchline: His trivial reason for being at the bank
jokes.explanation: Homer's self-centered panic combined with admission of his mundane bank visit reason.

jokes.text: Hey, if I can say yes to Chinese toys that kill kids, you can say yes to him.
jokes.setup: Krusty encouraging Marge to agree to visit the robber
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to endorsing dangerous toys
jokes.explanation: Dark contemporary reference to toy safety scandals. Perfect Krusty moral flexibility.

jokes.text: You said you visited him every night. / Moe the tavern, not Moe the person.
jokes.setup: Marge calling out Homer's lie about visiting sick Moe
jokes.punchline: Homer's technical truth about visiting the bar
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - technically truthful but morally empty. Excellent wordplay and character consistency.

jokes.text: Moe was in the hospital for three weeks, and I didn't visit him once. / You said you visited him every night. / Moe the tavern, not Moe the person.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming he doesn't owe people visits
jokes.punchline: Homer's visiting the bar, not the hospitalized person
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection revealing Homer's priorities and relationship with alcohol over people

jokes.text: Ooh! Shlomo's Judaica is having a blowout on dreidels.
jokes.setup: Homer agreeing Marge should visit the prisoner
jokes.punchline: Immediately distracted by random sale
jokes.explanation: Homer's ADHD-like distraction from serious moments. 'Blowout on dreidels' is specifically absurd.

jokes.text: I'm so nervous it feels like I keistered a bunch of butterflies.
jokes.setup: Dwight nervously waiting for Marge to visit prison
jokes.punchline: Prison slang version of 'butterflies in stomach'
jokes.explanation: Clever prison adaptation of common phrase. 'Keistered butterflies' is original and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: My real mother abandoned me in an amusement park. All I have left is a caricature from that day.
jokes.setup: Dwight's tragic backstory about mother abandonment
jokes.punchline: Only memento is cheesy carnival artwork
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy - tragic abandonment made pathetic by tacky souvenir detail

jokes.text: Listen carefully. Someone's been editing my biography on Wikipedia. I want you to kill him. / No problem, sweetie. I'll dump his body in the same lake as your lousy lawyer. / Then when you get out, we can have a picnic there! / Aw, that's so sweet.
jokes.setup: Mob boss giving murder orders during prison visit
jokes.punchline: Romantic picnic planning at body dump site
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy escalation - murder becomes romantic planning. Good contrast between violence and domesticity.

jokes.text: I want you to kill him. / No problem, sweetie. I'll dump his body in the same lake as your lousy lawyer. Then when you get out, we can have a picnic there!
jokes.setup: Casual prison murder request
jokes.punchline: Sweet domestic planning around multiple murder locations
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy escalation - romantic planning around serial killing

jokes.text: I really shouldn't show up without an apple pie for Dwight. In fact, I really shouldn't show up without an apple pie for everyone at the prison.
jokes.setup: Marge making excuses not to visit prison
jokes.punchline: Escalating to impossibly large baking project
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge logic - polite excuse escalates to absurd hospitality obligation

jokes.text: Hello, this is Ted Nugent, the Motor City Madman, urging you to vote no on Proposition 87. If we outlaw crossbows in our public schools, who's gonna protect our children from charging elk?
jokes.setup: Political robocall from Ted Nugent
jokes.punchline: Absurd scenario about elk attacking schools
jokes.explanation: Good satire of absurd political arguments and celebrity endorsements. Ted Nugent is perfect choice for this type of argument.

jokes.text: Hello, this is Ted Nugent, the Motor City Madman, urging you to vote no on Proposition 87. If we outlaw crossbows in our public schools, who's gonna protect our children from charging elk?
jokes.setup: Political robocall interrupting Marge's crisis
jokes.punchline: Absurd gun rights argument about elk in schools
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of extreme gun rights arguments with absurd wildlife threat

jokes.text: I ain't afraid of old sparky, the hot seat, the kilowatt couch, the death davenport, the electric lap, the crook cooker, the scorch stool!
jokes.setup: Gangster claiming he's not afraid of electric chair
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd synonyms for electric chair
jokes.explanation: Creative escalation of electric chair synonyms. 'Death davenport' and 'scorch stool' are particularly inventive.

jokes.text: Steroids cause health problems / Sluggers make millions.
jokes.setup: Itchy and Scratchy cartoon with social message
jokes.punchline: Simple cost-benefit analysis of steroid use
jokes.explanation: Sharp economic logic applied to sports ethics. Maximum economy in the punchline.

jokes.text: Who can it be knocking at my door? Make no sound Tiptoe across the floor If he hears, he'll knock all... Oh, hey, oh. Oh, wait, this ain't where I go to vote.
jokes.setup: Marge terrified of knock at door, singing Men at Work song
jokes.punchline: Opens to confused voter looking for polling place
jokes.explanation: Good tension-breaking misdirection with pop culture reference

jokes.text: They're so buggy, I can't.
jokes.setup: Dwight asking Marge to look him in the eyes
jokes.punchline: Marge unable to because of his bulging eyes
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier character description. Marge's honest discomfort is relatable.

jokes.text: I'd like to, but then you'll leave like she did. / Can you at least put it in your jacket? / Fine, but I'm still gonna make that pointy shape.
jokes.setup: Marge asking Dwight to get rid of his gun
jokes.punchline: Compromise of hiding gun while maintaining threatening gesture
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dwight's childlike need for control and Marge's reasonable negotiation.

jokes.text: Sorry I offered your life for my safety.
jokes.setup: Krusty acknowledging their shared bank robbery experience
jokes.punchline: Casual apology for betraying Marge during the robbery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty - casual acknowledgment of serious moral failure. Understated delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I saw it, I just didn't feel like talking to you.
jokes.setup: Someone claiming they predicted romantic spark between Marge and Dwight
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's antisocial honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy characterization - antisocial but observant. Direct and economical.

jokes.text: Can't a man spend one minute with his stuffed monkey? / One minute? / A monkey would make a better chief. / What did you say? / I said monkey would make a better chief!
jokes.setup: Wiggum distracted during hostage crisis by stuffed animal
jokes.punchline: Lou's insult escalating to shouting
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Lou's frustration with Wiggum's incompetence. Stuffed monkey detail is absurd.

jokes.text: I'll show them which monkey would make the best chief!
jokes.setup: Wiggum trying to prove himself during crisis
jokes.punchline: Taking Lou's monkey insult literally
jokes.explanation: Wiggum's literal interpretation of the insult drives his heroic action. Character-consistent stupidity.

jokes.text: Instead of a carnival ride, what if he was run over by a Zamboni with a moustache? / I don't see how. / Then I got nothing.
jokes.setup: Krusty trying to suggest alternative comedy scenarios
jokes.punchline: Completely random Zamboni idea, immediate abandonment when questioned
jokes.explanation: Absurdist comedy writing process - random elements with no logic. Krusty's immediate capitulation is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You chat up the guard at the gate, while I knife the guard behind me. / I can't do that, Dwight. / I figured you'd say that.
jokes.setup: Dwight giving Marge carved soap frame with inscription
jokes.punchline: Inscription is escape plan instructions, his acceptance of her refusal
jokes.explanation: Great subversion - romantic gesture becomes escape plan. Dwight's matter-of-fact acceptance adds character depth.
